mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in FEDFUNDS increases the discount rate applied to expected equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows FEDFUNDS=0.37 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth crosses above a strong 3-month threshold, household demand is accelerating and near-term earnings expectations improve, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows RSAFS=453438.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered RSAFS accelerations do not produce higher mean 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered months at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a 3-month threshold signals weakening labor income and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows UNRATE=5.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day S&P 500 returns after UNRATE increases above the threshold are positive in more than 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains nearly unchanged, inflation pressure rises without offsetting policy restraint, increasing future tightening risk and lowering S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month percent change crosses above +1.0% while FEDFUNDS 3-month change is below +0.25 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows CPIAUCSL=237.92 and FEDFUNDS=0.37
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger has no lower 20-day return distribution than CPIAUCSL acceleration alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in INDPRO signals improving production, operating leverage, and revenue momentum for cyclicals, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows INDPRO=103.3759 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are not associated with positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals falling construction activity and weakening interest-sensitive demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows HOUST=1089.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines below the threshold are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises strongly while UNRATE falls, labor income growth is broadening and default risk is declining, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change exceeds +300 while UNRATE 3-month change is below -0.2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows PAYEMS=143915.0 and UNRATE=5.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE joint trigger fails to outperform unconditional 20-day S&P 500 returns by at least 25 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly INDPRO decline below a negative threshold signals an immediate growth scare and downward earnings revision risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot shows INDPRO=103.3759 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day S&P 500 returns after INDPRO declines below the threshold are not lower than unconditional 5-day returns in walk-forward testing.
